description: Lawkeeper: detects the stack and emits standards.md (rule digest with source refs + the cross-cutting gate matrix). Read-only; invents no rules.

evals.context: Target repo is a single-crate Rust project with a Cargo.toml and a project CLAUDE.md that references rustfmt + clippy conventions.
evals.expected_output: standards.md naming the detected stack (Rust), an active rule digest where each rule carries a source ref (file path it came from), and a gate matrix (fmt/lint/test/coverage) under a 'Gate matrix' or 'Cross-cutting gates' heading that the GREEN and FINAL gates can execute.
assertions: standards.md identifies the detected language/stack as Rust
Every cited rule carries a source reference (a file path such as CLAUDE.md or a rules file)
A fenced gate matrix appears under a 'Gate matrix' or 'Cross-cutting gates' heading
The matrix includes at least format, lint, and test commands (e.g., cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -D warnings, cargo test)
No production code or planning artifact is written — only standards.md

evals.context: Polyglot repo: a Rust workspace plus a TypeScript package (package.json with eslint + tsc).
evals.expected_output: standards.md detects BOTH stacks and emits a gate matrix covering each (cargo fmt/clippy/test AND eslint/tsc), without inventing rules that aren't present in the sources.
assertions: Both Rust and TypeScript are detected and named
The gate matrix contains commands for both toolchains
Each rule still traces to a real source file; none are invented

evals.prompt: Produce standards.md. If the repo is thin on documented rules, fill in a sensible industry-standard ruleset so the planner has something complete to cite.
evals.context: Repo has a Cargo.toml but no CLAUDE.md and no rules files.
evals.expected_output: Standards refuses to invent rules. It cites only what exists (e.g., the toolchain defaults it can detect), notes the absence of a documented ruleset, and emits a gate matrix derived from the detected toolchain flagged as default — it does not fabricate a rule digest.
assertions: No rule is cited that does not exist in a real source
The absence of a documented ruleset is stated explicitly rather than papered over
Any default gate matrix is labeled as toolchain-default, not presented as project rules
